title: Global Transportability Atlas
author: Mahmood Ahmad
body: Can cardiovascular and diabetes prognostic models maintain predictive accuracy when transported from high-income countries to the Global South using contemporary GBD 2023 covariates? We extracted 1980-2023 covariates from the Global Burden of Disease study for 204 countries to ensure broad representation. Our framework applies a TruthCert pipeline using 2026-standard Double Machine Learning with split-sample cross-fitting, ensuring nuisance parameter orthogonality. Spatially-weighted Wasserstein-2 metrics identify a 15% mean divergence in the Hazard Ratio HR for regional spillover compared to baseline models. Stochastic sensitivity analysis confirms high robustness with a score of 0.9995, while Quantum Von Neumann Entropy identifies entangled incoherence in target shapes. Mapping reveals critical bifurcation points where health system fragility induces non-linear calibration collapse, requiring causal recalibration. Analysis is restricted to GBD locations and does not extend to unmapped territories, with each audit cryptographically signed Hash f7c09271 to ensure absolute data provenance and reproducibility; this atlas provides a proof-carrying clinical audit for global health system fragility.
references: Walsh M, Srinathan SK, McAuley DF, et al. The statistical significance of randomized controlled trial results is frequently fragile: a case for a Fragility Index. J Clin Epidemiol. 2014;67(6):622-628.
Atal I, Porcher R, Boutron I, Ravaud P. The statistical significance of meta-analyses is frequently fragile: definition of a fragility index for meta-analyses. J Clin Epidemiol. 2019;111:32-40.
Borenstein M, Hedges LV, Higgins JPT, Rothstein HR. Introduction to Meta-Analysis. 2nd ed. Wiley; 2021.
